reviews.review_text:
We stayed at the Cockatoo Island campground with the basic camping package, which included 2 sleeping mats and 2 camping chairs.
No tent setup was required, which made the experience much easier and convenient.
The camping site was good, and the facilities were better than we expected. There were hot water showers and toilets, a kitchen, BBQ area, fridge, microwave, toaster, seating area, outdoor cinema, and fire pit area. Tongs, dishwashing liquid, and paper towels were also provided at the BBQ area.
There are also two cafés and vending machines available on the island.

Parramatta and Lane Cove rivers
There around 1 hour 1 ferry from parramatta to cockatoo island, no fee for visitors

This must be one of Sydney's best kept secrets. I have lived in Sydney all my life and never really considered going there. We went for our Golden Wedding Anniversary and our children and grandchildren joined us. We stayed in the Harbour view apartments (3 Apartments in the one house) which are brilliant. Very well appointed. Uninterrupted views of the harbour, Sydney Harbour Bridge and the north shore. Amazing sunrise each morning and fun during the day just watching everything going on in the harbour. Very interesting to wander the island and learn of its many uses over the years starting with being a convict penal settlement and moving on later to be a major ship building infrastructure. Staff were friendly and very helpful.
Great view, peaceful surroundings with a nice apartment
Glamping experience. Perfect view near the sea shore, Harbour bridge. Million dollar view.

Great location , amazing camp Kitchen. Only a quick ferry ride and super peaceful with beautiful views. We really enjoyed our tent. It was already made up or we had to do was bring food. I would however recommend they build a family friendly attraction. Maybe a playground or a pool?
Excellent! An inner city adventure.

Lovely overnight stay in the Firestation studio. Very spacious.  Wonderful to be in the centre of the harbour watching passing ferries and afternoon yachties sail by. The social history is so interesting and the massive pieces of machinery amazing. Even a day visit would  be filled with exploring

Cockatoo Island was amazing. We camped in the pre-set up tents for one night. It was a 14th birthday party - 5 teenagers, 2 adults supervising. The teens had a great time playing hide and seek and exploring the island, particularly the Turbine Hall. BBQ facilities were excellent and well-provisioned (paper towel, dish washing liquid, tongs, microwaves, fridges, toasters). Bathrooms were very clean and well-lit. The showers had great water pressure and were private.
Fantastic place for a short break.  Lots to do and see. Facilities were clean and well maintained.

We stayed in a waterfront tent. It had a comfortable stretcher bedding, towels and chairs. The showers and toilets are modern and clean and the camp kitchen is very good.

The service of the staff is excellent and we enjoyed our stay.

The island itself is very interesting and provides an excellent overview of the different periods in Sydney’s history.

Really interesting place to explore. Some amazing abandoned industrial archeology plus really interesting stuff about the past use a a jail. Very quiet when I visited in early evening, plus it’s free!

Glamping at Cockatoo Island is an unforgettable experience. I've taken so many people there - I've camped with our kids, with friends from interstate and with my partner. It's so easy to get there (15 minutes on the ferry from Circular Quay!), and the camping packages make it incredibly easy to stay with very little effort. ||Most people bring food to BBQ and some amazing meals get made by groups in the camp kitchen, which is equipped with multiple free gas bbqs, fridges, zip boiler and microwave. For this recent visit we didn't even pack food - buying lunch (pizza) at one of the two island cafes, then two wraps which we toasted on the free, immaculately clean BBQs for dinner later. Note that the cafes close early, nothing open in the evening so check the hours on the day you visit and plan accordingly. ||My partner and I brought our bikes to the island this visit, and rode around the island freely, covering corners we hadn't recahed before, which was a new experience and highly recommended. It's easy to take bikes on the ferry, no extra charge. Helmets are required by law. ||I recommend the Deluxe Waterfront camping package - the small 'waterfront' premium gives you a perfect spot, plus a lantern, which makes it perfect. And you're in the middle of Sydney harbour, which is magical at night.
Cockatoo Island is amazing and it provides a simple accommodation great for one night if you want to be minimalist. If you stay longer you’d need to bring more essentials

Good Location, but Cleanliness and Amenities Need Improvement||The location is beautiful and the atmosphere has great potential, especially being on an island. However, the accommodation did not meet basic expectations. The room (tent) was not properly prepared for hot weather, with no fan, and the pillows, towels, and bedding were not changed despite staying more than one day. Cleanliness was also an issue, particularly in the bathrooms. Noise levels were acceptable, but the lack of amenities such as mosquito control and designated swimming areas was disappointing.|Service was mixed: the security staff were excellent—very respectful, professional, and understanding—and one staff member who brought ice was helpful. Unfortunately, the staff member who handled my complaint did not take it seriously. Overall, the experience could be much better with improved cleanliness, better facilities, and more attentive services

We stayed here during a rainy weekend and still had a great experience. The tent was small but really comfortable and warm. The location is perfect — lots to see and do, and the ferry makes it super easy to get around. Onsite security made us feel safe overnight. Don’t forget to bring your own cookware if you’re self-catering!
Location is great, very noisy site, cockroaches in the premium water front tent which is around $400 per night, bed is really small, we had hard time to rest, not really recommended.

Views are amazing.
Kitchen is very well set out. Heaps of seating & boiling water is available 24/7
For a group of 70 + year old ladies- toilets were a but too far for nocturnal episodes over wet grass & steps.
Tents were great - warm & cosy but a bit too low & thin for older ladies. 1 lady left. Dismal & wet this morning in the thick fog.
Great access to Woolwich Pier Hotel & Sydney Rowers club at Abbotsford via ferry.
Down side: many feral roaming students from Canberra out of their area loud & noisy & disrespectful - perhaps out of control.?
Good side: meeting delightful 15 year old students from Charters Towers Qld - a boarding school with delightful respectful students - a credit to the staff & their school.

Cons:you can’t do fishing and there isn’t that much entertainment for the kids.
Pro’s : you can go to water grill in a Jeffy and it is good for one day camping.

Stayed here on a wet and cold weekend and whilst the weather did dampen things, we had a great time. Tent was tight for space, but comfortable and warm. Location is fantastic and there is so much to explore. Easy access via the ferry and felt very safe overnight with onsite security.
BYO cooking pots, utensils, plates etc if self catering.

skvělý stylový camp v podobě vojenských stanů. Součástí je zázemí velké kuchyně, kde můžete vařit, posezení, sprchy, prádelna, program v podobě letního kina. Je to netradiční a zábavné. Ubytovaní návštěvníci mají vyhrazený prostor, takže jednodenní turisté je nijak nemusí obtěžovat.

Incredible view from the house balcony~|The house so clean and cozy ~|The staffs are helpful.|Love the decoration on this place.|It’s good to have a good time a fun time with friends and families!!!!|We will come back again for sure!!!!!!!!!!!

fantastic island.
i hoped for more kids activities during this easter break time. the cafe/restaurants closing at 6:30pm was a bit silly.
$150/ night to sleep on the floor due to a faulty sleeping mat is too expensive.
considering they did not clean the tent beforehand, arrange any furniture, check the sleeping mats, provide any bedding, or really do anything for the accommodation tent itself; it seems a little pricey.

Reasonably priced campsite a ferry ride away from the city centre. The island is an interesting oasis away from the hustle of the city.

Facilities are mediocre. 'Kitchen' is just some barbecue grills and a fridge. Anyone can enter the campsite and there were quite some people on the island till late who didn't sleep there. And I have no idea who thought it would be a good idea for byo tents to set up on the only field which has a spotlight on it.

We stayed last Saturday night in a Deluxe tent. Whilst the tent was adequate, the tent itself was very hot and did not cool down till 3am. The ghost tour was not running due to music on the grass at the top of Cockatoo island- At 35.00 entry per person!|If it's raining, there is next to no shelter. certainly not enough for all who are staying overnight.|There are 2 cafes. Be aware hours vary on the day of the week|One on the southern side of the Island open from 9am till 5pm - with last kitchen orders at 4pm.|One on the northern side of the Island open 830am till 8pm - last kitchen orders at 7pm. We arrived at this cafe at 615pm, only to be told they were busy cooking pre orders (which we were not aware was a thing, nor were we told during the inductions- if an induction is what you could call it after checking in) which would take till 7pm. THEREFORE, they were NOT TAKING ANY MORE FOOD ORDERS. the only available food being muffins, banana bread and biscuits,|They were still selling alcohol in contraindication of Resonsible Service of Alcohol which states you should sell food with Alcohol.|Luckily, we had taken cheese and biscuits for snacks which became out dinner.|Yes, you can BBQ but staying one night we decided we didn't want the hassle of carrying food and cooking utensils and were happy to buy food but none was available as stated above.|Due a bad night sleep due to heat and children awake and running around at 6am, and yes, we did expect noise and limited sleep. But we did hope for coffee and breakfast. HOWEVER, the cafe did not open till 830am for coffee or breakfast. So once again, NO coffee or breakfast for us. We caught the 828am Ferry out of there.|If you like camping and taking all your food for 1 night, then this is probably for you.|BUT if you want the experience without the hassle of carting all your food, and think you can buy it, I will say forget it.|I have had friends ask for my advice and after advising the same, they have decided to forget the night of camping and opt for a day trip instead.

Ridiculously noisy with seagulls, campsite runner quite unfriendly, was reluctant to let us camp anywhere but essentially by the seagull nesting ground (because that area was reserved for luxury pre set up camping).

All in all quite expensive for not a lot.
Good place.Good views
This is the second time we’ve camped here and it was fantastic! The luxury waterfront tents are exactly what I want out of camping!
It was a great place, went there with the family and I enjoyed it to the moon and back it has so many historical places

Went here for a school trip..actaul island was amazing
TENTS-7/10-small
FOOD-2/10
STAFF-8/10
One tour guide was so nice..very amazing..Forgot his name lol
Other guests where very rude

I would like to thank the responsible management because it is truly a very, very wonderful place. I was impressed by the cleanliness—clean kitchen, clean corridors, clean sheets—and the peace and respect. It is worth visiting, and I encourage everyone to visit and try camping. However, there is one downside: the café at the entrance is not up to the level of service at all. There are mixed odors and a bit of chaos. When you want to enjoy a cup of coffee, you find yourself sitting next to the smell of frying oil and eggs 🥴, and the liquor bottles on the shelf don’t look good, especially with children around. I hope the situation at the café changes, or that it gets divided into more elegant sections. Another downside is that it wasn’t clear exactly what time the outdoor cinema would start or what movie would be shown. Lastly, the women’s restroom is not cleaned regularly, which is somewhat annoying.

This place has the worst customer service and pricing structure possible. They rob you wherever they can.
We had to change our booking due to a mistake in room type on our end, and you are charged $20 for an “administrative fee”, which I’m sure any person with 1hour of training could do without effort.
When I called to mention my mistake, as soon as I mentioned the word “cancel” the tone of the person on the other end of the line drastically changed to the most appalling and rude one ever.

Separately, if you want to rebook, you are charged 20% on top of the price if you want to book on a Saturday. This is ridiculous!! I’m sure they do this to make it look more expensive and so that people just make a new booking instead of using the credit, so they end up keeping your money.

The location itself is great, but all these other problems surrounding it just make it a terrible experience overall.
Great location and fantastic history, nice cafes etc. We did not camp but stayed in one of the residence's. It is worth a weekend away, not cheap but worth it.

Disappointed +++
Front of house staff were rude and dismissive.
Walked into campsite to an open tampon on the ground out front of tent. Our toddler picked up a bolt from the ground - clearly from the deck area.
Rained overnight and despite being zipped up tight 2 out of 4 of us awoke wet and cold.
Quiet time after 10 was not enforced and a large group were disruptive and rude.
Toilet floor was hosed at night with no wet signs - absolutely disgusting and dangerous to all. Toilet doors broken. Toilets blocked.
BBQ area was overrun with rude other guests - we ended up getting first ferry out and taking our pre ordered food home.

We will not return or recommend.

The only positive was the location and the time we spent as a family doing a self guided tour.
Avoid the cafe at all costs, possibly the worst coffee I have ever tasted & not worth the 40 minute wait Sunday morning!

Beautiful place for the weekend gateway. Clean and well maintained, staff at the visitor centre and the security very nice. It's nice to walk around the island and enjoy the beautiful sunset and sunrise.

The place is so majestic. Perfect place to relax.
Just a side comment about the receptionist who attended us, she’s a bit rude though. It seems like we are not welcome when we arrived. Even when we bought a pack of ice, she just threw on the ground. Why don’t just give it nicely.

A really luxurious glamping experience! Tents were really clean, cozy and comfy, and the campsite had a really beautiful vibe to it. The camp also featured a large kitchen (with BBQ's, microwaves and outlets), a big field (to throw the footy on) and a large, clean shower block. Would highly recommend!
Good campsite and very near Sydney. The price is worth it. I had a good time with a beautiful sunset.

城市露營來說蠻方便的
但房間到晚上超暗 沒有插座
要走去廚房那邊 風景不錯
帳篷位置要慎選
不然走到浴室超遠
晚上蠻吵的 有直升機🚁 船🚤 對岸還有人在嗨 各種鳥🐦合唱  飛這麼遠來 有趣的體驗啦

Appalling customer service.
They were unable to uphold my booking despite receiving a confirmation email due to a known website glitch that they tried to blame on me.
When I asked for a refund I was fobbed off, passed on to the manager and treated in the most condescending way. They never acknowledged their mistake and I am still left with a credit rather than a refund.

The customer service so bad i cnt use any words.
We needed someone's to help us to book a site over the phone . They keep saying go online we said we cnt do booking online for some reason. Can you guys help us book it over the phone  theybsaid no we can not help you. I dnt understand why, very useless people and not happy at all
Please people don't waste your time booking with them.
Appartments were very stylish, co.fortable and clean. Excellent view and easy walking around.

We stayed in our own tent and enjoyed it immensely. The facilities are lovely, with a great camp kitchen (there is a smaller kitchen in the byo tent area that was very useful, with instant boiling water, 2 bbqs, sink & microwave). We had it to ourselves for 2 of our 3 nights. There are members of the public exploring the island during the day but there are rangers and security around too, which made it feel secure. There were a few school groups staying but they were very quiet, a real credit to their schools. We also enjoyed the films at night. You can catch a ferry to be back in the city, but it felt great to be in a peaceful spot (except for the seagulls. Take earplugs). Great sunrises and sunsets. Absolutely loved it, my favourite camping experience in Australia.
It's beautiful,serene ,historical place .Nice for picnic ,family visit or Night Camping .

We stayed here in the Duluxe waterfront tent and it was great view. Unfortunately we went on a cold and windy night but the tent held out and didn't spring any leaks! The tent was set up well and clean and so was all the facilities for the kitchen. We went to watch a movie at night too so that was fun. Will definitely come back and hope for better weather.
Cool camping 🏕 experience, it's a little bit pricey tho, but the scenery makes up for it. Sometimes it can b a bit busy with tourist's try early in the week and it's not so busy...
Was there for a tour and it was a quiet and relaxing place.
Lugar maravilhoso
Amazing place

A nice island in the middle of Sydney, Cockatoo island offered both historical/paranormal tour and overnight camping at pre-determined locations near the wharf. The tents were nicely setup and you can request for extra services and bbq supplies during your stay. This is the perfect location for a quick weekend retreat and have outdoor BBQ with friends and family.

Amazing amazing camping site. There is communal kitchen, campfire and sitting area. One of the amazing sunset spot in Sydney. We stayed for a night. Highly recommended. You can even camp with your own tents. Historic island and many horror stories. We even went ghost haunting but was no luck. It’s an amazing weekend gateway. You have to take wharf to the island.
